items.title: U.S. job creation cools in June with payrolls growth of just 57,000; unemployment rate at 4.2%
items.description: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to rise by 115,000 in June and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.3%.

items.title: Rivian raises 2026 delivery outlook while Lucid misses Wall Street expectations for second quarter
items.description: Rivian is increasing its delivery outlook to between 65,000 and 70,000 EVs, up from 62,000 to 67,000 units.

items.title: Ford Q2 sales drop 10.3% due to F-Series production issues, falling EV demand
items.description: Ford said its EV sales fell by 40.7% during the quarter compared with a year earlier, while sales of its F-Series trucks, including the F-150, fell 11%.

items.title: Most prediction market contracts have low volume, leaving users exposed to volatility and bots
items.description: Prediction market volume has grown exponentially but several markets never make it over $10,000.

items.title: Jeff Bezos' family office backed five AI startups in June
items.description: Bezos Expeditions is now the most active family office investor thus far this year, per data from Fintrx.

items.title: Tesla reports 480,126 vehicle deliveries for second quarter, topping expectation
items.description: Tesla is trying to recover from consecutive annual declines in vehicle sales that were partly caused by a consumer backlash against CEO Elon Musk.

items.title: Russia’s neighbor to scrap ban on nuclear weapons, says ‘situation is getting worse’
items.description: The decision comes shortly after lawmakers in Finland voted to lift its longstanding ban on nuclear weapons.

items.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Thursday
items.description: The June jobs report came in weak, and Palantir landed a big upgrade.

items.title: Microsoft commits $2.5 billion and 6,000 employees to new AI implementation unit
items.description: Microsoft is the latest tech company to form a business focused on helping customers understand and implement artificial intelligence.

items.title: Oil prices fall after Qatar says U.S. and Iran made progress during talks
items.description: Oil prices fell more than 1% on Wednesday after talks between Washington and Tehran concluded.

items.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
items.description: A historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

items.title: High-end camping and a capital raise: AutoCamp is banking on summer travel to fuel growth
items.description: AutoCamp offers Airstream suites, polished cabins, fire pits, design-forward amenities and access to iconic outdoor destinations.

items.title: Meta’s push into cloud computing means Wall Street has to prepare for lower margins
items.description: Meta appears poised to enter the cloud computing market in an effort to monetize its massive AI infrastructure.

items.title: Brookfield wants to build AI data centers in London’s answer to Wall Street
items.description: Brookfield's CEO said the firm is bringing data centers to London's Canary Wharf, as demand for AI infrastructure in the U.K. soars.

items.title: Ford CEO wants level playing field with Toyota, GM imports as USMCA trade talks reopen
items.description: Ford reports it assembled more than 2 million vehicles in the U.S. last year – more than any other auto manufacturer, including 311,000 units for export.

items.title: Google loses fight over record $4.7 billion EU antitrust fine
items.description: In 2018, the European Commission slapped Google with the record-breaking penalty on the grounds that it abused Android's mobile dominance.

items.title: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration to ease Washington pressure: Report
items.description: Trump said in June that the U.S. taking an ownership stake in AI giants would be "a beautiful thing" and make American public "partners in this revolution."

items.title: Americans are paying record prices for steak. Here's why demand isn't cracking
items.description: Despite record beef prices, Americans aren't giving up steak. Consumers are treating beef as an affordable luxury and prioritizing it for special occasions.

items.title: SpaceX may emerge as ultimate blueprint for new wave of mega-cap IPOs
items.description: "It would not surprise me at all to see a similar dynamic play out with some of these [IPOs] set to come in the months ahead," the Kathmere Capital CIO says.

items.title: AI agents will soon be able to match human traders, Robinhood CEO tells CNBC
items.description: Vlad Tenev spoke about the potential of AI agents in trading in an interview with CNBC.
